title: Syracuse political infighting likely to continue as contentious city budget is finalized
summary: Syracuse Common Council unanimously overrode all 45 of Mayor Ben Walsh's budget vetoes on May 27, 2025, rejecting his proposed property tax increase and $27 million reserve fund draw. The contentious process—featuring closed-door executive sessions and a third-party cost-cutting report kept from public review—has triggered transparency concerns from open government advocates and sparked escalating personal conflict between Walsh and council leaders ahead of the 2025 mayoral election.

political_signal.description: Council leadership (Hogan, Majok) directly opposed Mayor Walsh's fiscal approach and budget priorities. Three council members (Hogan, Majok, Owens) are competing to succeed Walsh in 2025 mayoral race, suggesting budget conflict has electoral dimensions beyond governance.
